name: known-issues-batch-pass-registry
description: In-memory registry of issues confirmed earlier in the same run_batch_once pass, so a later flow's manager gets told 'flow X found this a moment ago' — context only, never a skip. Deliberately not indexed by container (container overlap is an unreliable relevance signal; the manager decides relevance itself) and deliberately never persisted across poll cycles (the demo app's source isn't git-checkpointed, so code can't be assumed unchanged between runs).
invariants: record_issue() is the only place entries are added, and it logs at the append itself so JSONL line order is the true chronology
An issue is only recorded when compute_fingerprint(anomaly) != 'clean' — LLM prose alone can't create a known issue
contracts: compute_fingerprint() is a readable sorted pipe-joined string (not a hash) over http/errlog/slow signals; 'clean' means no signals
note_for(registry) returns None for an empty registry, else one string covering all recorded entries
